text.title: Multi Product Portfolio Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Multi Product Portfolio Management application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each product MUST carry exactly one mode tag (build / grow / maintain / sunset); 'idle' or 'paused' are rejected — collapse to maintain.
rule.rationale: Four modes cover the lifecycle; extra modes drift into 'limbo' status.

rule.statement: Each mode MUST carry a weekly time cap (build ≤20h, grow ≤8h, maintain ≤2h, sunset ≤1h); over-cap is rejected.
rule.rationale: Time-cap forces explicit trade-offs across products instead of one product silently consuming the week.

rule.statement: Each product MUST carry an explicit capital allocation (USD/mo) — including $0 — never 'whatever's left'.
rule.rationale: Implicit capital allocation = whichever product has the loudest expense pays nothing back.

rule.statement: Portfolio MUST carry a cross-product kill rule (e.g. 'sunset mode for ≥3 quarters → archive'); products with no kill rule are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without kill rule, sunset-mode products linger as zombies in the config.

rule.statement: Portfolio config MUST carry a quarterly review date on calendar; ad-hoc reviews are rejected.
rule.rationale: Quarterly cadence catches mode drift before it becomes a year-long zombie problem.
